title: 20. Population variant distribution — pathogenic load vs truncation boundary ClinVar · 427 variants
desc: If the N-terminal is functionally critical, pathogenic variants should cluster there. We queried ClinVar (427 STRC variants) and mapped pathogenic/likely pathogenic variants across the protein. The first-pass Mini-STRC boundary (700) captures 87 % of the pathogenic load. The Ultra-Mini boundary (1075) retains the deep C-terminal core where the binding interface and E1659A reside, and trades ~5 LRR-region pathogenic variants for 1,125 bp of AAV headroom — acceptable because Ultra-Mini delivers wildtype protein and those variants cease to matter once the replacement protein is expressed.
retained_miss: 5+ (L714P — Mini only · W1475C, M1483R, P1520R, T1709A — both)
conclusion: 87% of pathogenic load is in the retained region. The N-terminal carries only 2 pathogenic missense variants — and even these may be pseudogene contamination artifacts, since exons 1-15 share 100% identity between STRC and its pseudogene STRCP1. Nonsense variants in the N-terminal (positions 87-410) are pathogenic because they also truncate the C-terminal — they do not prove N-terminal function per se.
caveat: Pseudogene caveat: STRCP1 shares >99% coding sequence with STRC. Exons 1-15 (entire N-terminal) have 100% identity — short-read sequencing cannot distinguish the two. N-terminal variant counts in ClinVar and gnomAD may include pseudogene contamination. Only long-read sequencing or long-range PCR reliably calls variants in this region.
bhatt: Independent validation: Bhatt et al. (dual-AAV STRC, Science Advances 2022) chose their split point at Ser746/Cys747 to "avoid predicted structural domains" — almost exactly the first-pass Mini-STRC boundary (700). Ultra-Mini takes the next step past the LRR linker (1075) and is validated directly by AF3 interface preservation at the TMEM145 binding pocket (§9–§11).
